PHASE: (dev-app-redis-url) ‚Äî COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `3677af2` (implementation branch was `1fa4cb5`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch ‚Äî 1 tracked file / 6 insertions / 1 deletion + 1 gitignored env regen). SCOPE EXPANDED mid-ticket from REDIS_URL-only to REDIS_URL + Prisma binary because the ticket's stated value ("unblock browser smoke for UI work") required fixing BOTH stacked blockers ‚Äî REDIS_URL alone would have left the app crashing on first DB query. Same in-scope-by-necessity pattern as [[coturn-config-fix]] precedent. ROOT CAUSE BUG A (REDIS_URL): Phase 3 generated REDIS_PASSWORD via `openssl rand -base64 32 | head -c 22` which can include `/+=` (base64 alphabet); the password landed as `Ro2JxvBIBJ/aEkhvALnFB3` containing a literal `/`. `deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml:34` constructs `REDIS_URL: redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@${COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME}_valkey:6379` by raw string interpolation ‚Äî the `/` breaks the URL into user:pass@host + path, failing WHATWG URL parse + Zod's `.url()` validation at instrumentation-hook load. ROOT CAUSE BUG B (Prisma): After clearing Bug A, app crashed on `PrismaClientInitializationError: Prisma Client could not locate the Query Engine for runtime "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"` ‚Äî host (WSL2 debian/glibc) generates a debian-only Prisma client, Dockerfile uses node:22-alpine (musl), the .prisma/client copied from host build context has the wrong .so.node. FIX (2 changes): (a) regenerated REDIS_PASSWORD in .env.dev (gitignored) via `openssl rand -hex 16` ‚Üí 32-char URL-safe hex; updated REDIS_URL field in lockstep. Secret values never entered AI context (sed flow with `unset` at end). (b) added `binaryTargets = ["native", "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"]` to `generator client` in packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma; `pnpm db:generate` produced both `libquery_engine-debian-openssl-3.0.x.so.node` AND `libquery_engine-linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x.so.node` in .prisma/client. Prisma picks the right one at runtime per process.platform + libc detection. VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev down && up -d` brought stack up healthy (one transient `docker builder prune -f` needed to clear a snapshot-extraction cache corruption during the first up attempt ‚Äî unrelated to either fix); `docker logs yelli_dev_app` shows `‚úì Starting...` + `‚úì Ready in 243ms` + no Invalid-server-env crashes + no Prisma engine errors; `curl http://localhost:43512/` ‚Üí HTTP 200 with 13.6kB HTML; grep of rendered HTML confirmed all 5 expected unauthed-state strings present ("Yelli", "Real-time team comms", "Get started", "Sign in", "Powerbyte") ‚Äî no "Go to app" (correct, unauthenticated request). pnpm test 192/192 ‚úì (unchanged); pnpm typecheck ‚úì clean; pnpm build ‚úì 22 routes unchanged. TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS ‚Äî ticket goal "unblock browser smoke for UI work" satisfied (HTTP 200 + expected landing content rendered + Prisma client loads cleanly); scope expansion was in-scope-by-necessity per [[coturn-config-fix]] precedent. Stage 2 quality PASS ‚Äî single-line schema edit + URL-safe password regen, no `any` types added, no scope creep beyond what's needed to boot the app, conventional commit ahead. 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: ‚öñÔ∏è [[url-unsafe-chars-in-interpolated-passwords]] documenting (a) base64 `/+=` √ó inline-compose-URL-interpolation footgun, (b) recovery pattern (URL-safe hex regen), (c) Phase 3 generation policy gap (passwords feeding URLs must be URL-safe ‚Äî either openssl rand -hex, or strip `/+=` from base64), (d) Prisma musl binary as a closely-related Alpine deployment gotcha, (e) STACKED-BUG RULE codifying when to expand a ticket's scope (small + masked + delivery requires both). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries ‚Äî defect fix, not architectural decision (though the future Phase 3 policy IS a candidate for a follow-up DECISIONS_LOG entry once implemented). BONUS SIDE-EFFECT: this session also confirmed the previous (root-landing-page) ticket renders correctly in the browser ‚Äî that ticket's smoke was blocked then, now done. NEW QUEUE ITEMS (NOT fixed this ticket): (a) Phase 3 password generation spec ‚Äî mandate URL-safe characters for any password that feeds into a URL via raw compose interpolation; affects future projects bootstrapped from this framework. (b) Compose interpolation pattern ‚Äî generalize to reference `${REDIS_URL_DOCKER}` from .env.* (where URL encoding is explicit + verifiable) instead of constructing inline; touches 9 compose files across dev/stage/prod for redis/postgres/etc. (c) Staging/prod env audit ‚Äî gitignored, can't verify from here, but the same /+= class issue likely affects them; recommend fresh URL-safe regen during next deploy. SKIPPED bootstrap + vercel-storage skill suggestions ‚Äî Prisma binaryTargets is standard Prisma API, not Vercel-specific. END STATE: dev environment fully restored; browser smoke now reliable for all UI tickets going forward; Phase 7 UI loop unblocked. Closes (dev-app-redis-url) from queue. Squash-merge pending.

PHASE_PRIOR: (root-landing-page) ‚Äî COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `4c64b2d` (implementation branch was `ee571b8`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch ‚Äî 3 files / ~118 net insertions). User selected "Minimal stub" + "Show landing + Go-to-app CTA" via AskUserQuestion before any code. SYMPTOM: prior `apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` was a 10-line auth-redirect (self-hosted style ‚Äî `if (session?.user) redirect("/app"); else redirect("/login")`); never matched PRODUCT.md line 233 (`/` = "Public landing page (SaaS) or login redirect (self-hosted)"). Yelli is the SaaS deployment, so a real public landing was the correct surface. FIX (3 files): (a) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/landing/cta.ts` (~28 lines) ‚Äî pure helper `getLandingCTAs({ isAuthed }): { primary, secondary? }` returning plain-data CTA descriptors (no functions, no JSX ‚Üí safe for Server‚ÜíClient serialization if extended later). Authed: `{ primary: { href:"/app", label:"Go to app" } }` only. Unauthed: `{ primary: { href:"/register", label:"Get started" }, secondary: { href:"/login", label:"Sign in" } }`. (b) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/landing/cta.test.ts` (~50 lines, 6 RED‚ÜíGREEN) ‚Äî happy paths + plain-data shape + funnel-priority guards (locks primary=/register-not-/login for unauthed; locks authed primary=/app-not-/login). (c) REPLACED page.tsx with public landing ‚Äî Server Component, awaits `auth()` once, calls `getLandingCTAs({ isAuthed: Boolean(session?.user) })`, renders header (Yelli wordmark + nav with secondary + primary CTAs) + hero (h1 + lede + duplicated CTAs as primary actions) + thin footer (¬© 2026 Powerbyte). Uses `<Button asChild>` + `<Link>` from `@yelli/ui/button` + `next/link` per established login-page convention. Tailwind utility classes only; shadcn/ui CSS variables for color tokens (Rule 21 graceful degradation ‚Äî no design-system/MASTER.md present, falls back to shadcn neutral defaults). VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 192/192 ‚úì (was 186, +6 new for getLandingCTAs); pnpm lint ‚úì 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff unchanged: app/layout.tsx no-css-tags + calls.test.ts non-null assertion); pnpm typecheck ‚úì clean; pnpm build ‚úì 22 routes + middleware 141kB unchanged + `/` route now ∆í 1.75kB / 115kB first-load (was a dynamic redirect with same SSR posture ‚Äî now serves HTML instead of issuing 307). BROWSER SMOKE: BLOCKED on PRE-EXISTING dev infra issue ‚Äî `yelli_dev_app` container was already in `Up 2h (unhealthy)` state before this session began, failing instrumentation hook with `Invalid server environment variables: REDIS_URL: Invalid url`. ALL routes (/, /login, /api/health) currently return HTTP 500 from the container ‚Äî not from my landing change (the container is running a stale build that pre-dates my code AND the env validation crashes BEFORE any route handler is invoked). Risk-mitigated for sign-off by: (a) `pnpm build` compilation success, (b) full unit coverage of the only branching logic (CTA selection ‚Äî 6 RED‚ÜíGREEN), (c) Server Component with NO client interactivity / NO client-side data fetching, (d) Tailwind utility classes only (no custom CSS / no new dependencies), (e) `<Button asChild>` + Link pattern already battle-tested across /login, /register, /forgot-password. TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS ‚Äî PRODUCT.md `/` is Public + matches user-confirmed scope (hero + two CTAs + footer + authed-show-Go-to-app over force-redirect, Vercel/Stripe pattern); Stage 2 quality PASS ‚Äî zero `any` types added; TDD RED‚ÜíGREEN evidenced (6 tests failed first then all passed after implementation); scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan (helper + test + page); no scope creep ‚Äî no marketing depth, no shadcnblocks pulled in, no new packages; conventional commit message ahead; shadcn primitives only (UI Rule #1). 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: üî¥ [[dev-app-redis-url-invalid]] documenting the `Up (unhealthy)` + uniform-HTTP-500 + instrumentation-crash pattern and the `docker logs ... | grep -B2 -A8 "Invalid server"` recovery diagnostic. 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries ‚Äî feature addition, not architectural decision. NEW QUEUE FINDING (NOT fixed in this ticket): (dev-app-redis-url) ‚Äî yelli_dev_app instrumentation hook fails with REDIS_URL: Invalid url; likely .env.dev value clobbered by an unrelated session OR env.ts schema tightened beyond the .env.dev format. Recovery path: compare .env.dev REDIS_URL against env.ts Zod schema, fix the value or relax the schema, restart container. PHASE_PRIOR: (t-slug-dev-routes-broken) ‚Äî COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `4142f79` (implementation branch was `038ebe7`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch ‚Äî 3 files / 146 insertions / 8 deletions). Symptom: `/t/{slug}/<anything>` always 404'd on localhost dev. Root cause: middleware.ts extracted `tenantSlug` correctly into `x-tenant-slug` header but NEVER stripped the `/t/{slug}` prefix from the path ‚Üí Next.js route resolution searched for a non-existent `/t/[slug]/app/...` handler tree. Bug was invisible to existing tests because `buildTenantRedirectUrl` tests only assert URL strings, never serve a request through the middleware ‚Üí route stack. FIX (3 files): (a) NEW pure helper `stripTenantPathPrefix(path, slug)` in `apps/web/src/server/tenant-redirect.ts` (~25 lines ‚Äî uses `path.match()` with regex-escaped slug; returns canonical path or original on no-match; defensive against regex-special-char slugs even though upstream validates `[a-z0-9-]+`). (b) 13 new RED‚ÜíGREEN tests in `tenant-redirect.test.ts` covering happy path + single segment + no-trailing ‚Üí "/" + /-trailing ‚Üí "/" + admin/superadmin/api paths + no-/t/ no-op + empty-slug no-op + slug-mismatch no-op + /tenants/* non-match + /t/yelli-other non-match + regex-special-char defensive. Total file: 26 tests. (c) middleware.ts wiring: compute `effectivePath = stripTenantPathPrefix(path, tenantSlug ?? "")` ONCE; use it for `isProtected` (fixes silent pass-through on unauthenticated /t/yelli/app/foo), `resolveTenantRedirect.path` (fixes /superadmin bypass not matching dev URLs), and the rewrite call itself. When `wasPathStripped`, return `NextResponse.rewrite(rewriteUrl, { request: { headers: requestHeaders } })` so existing /app, /admin, /superadmin handlers serve the request. Headers (x-tenant-slug + x-user-id + x-organization-id + x-organization-slug) DO survive `NextResponse.rewrite` when passed via the `{ request: { headers } }` option. `callbackUrl` on the unauthenticated-redirect path preserves the ORIGINAL `/t/{slug}/...` so login lands the user back on the same tenant-prefixed URL. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 186/186 ‚úì (was 173, +13 new); pnpm typecheck ‚úì 0 errors; pnpm lint ‚úì 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff: app/layout.tsx no-css-tags + calls.test.ts non-null assertion ‚Äî both predate this ticket); pnpm build ‚úì 22 routes + middleware 141kB UNCHANGED (MANDATORY per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] ‚Äî middleware.ts is THE Edge surface). Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS ‚Äî all 4 path families verified (/t/yelli/app, /admin, /superadmin, /api/*) rewrite to canonical paths; headers preserved; tenant cross-check uses effective path so /superadmin bypass works on dev URLs; auth callback URL preserves original /t/{slug}/... prefix; Stage 2 quality PASS ‚Äî zero `any` types added; TDD RED‚ÜíGREEN evidenced (13 tests failed first then passed after implementation); scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan (helper, test, middleware); no scope creep; conventional commit message ahead. SKIPPED routing-middleware skill's `proxy.ts` rename suggestion ‚Äî out of scope (Next.js 16 migration is a separate ticket; project is on 15). 1 new typed lesson: üü° [[t-slug-dev-routes-broken]] documenting the "middleware extracts BUT doesn't rewrite ‚Üí pure tests pass but 404 in browser" anti-pattern + heuristic ("if pure-function tests pass but the route still 404s, the integration layer is the gap ‚Äî pure tests cover decision logic, NOT request serving"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries ‚Äî defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: /t/{slug}/* dev URL pattern now works end-to-end. (#7c) subdomain redirect smoke is unblocked from the path-pattern dev URL side; still subject to having multi-user rig for the actual cross-tenant redirect scenarios per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

PHASE_PRIOR: (coturn-config-fix) ‚Äî COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged 75ab34f. Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch ‚Äî total diff is 3 YAML lines removed across dev/stage/prod docker-compose.media.yml + 3 env values filled in gitignored .env.dev/.env.staging/.env.prod + 1 new section added to gitignored CREDENTIALS.md; nowhere near subagent dispatch threshold). TWO LATENT BUGS were jointly blocking coturn (not just one as STATE.md previously suggested): (a) `COTURN_STATIC_AUTH_SECRET` in .env.dev was the literal placeholder string `devturnsecret_replace_with_real_value` ‚Äî coturn accepts it (no length validation on the HMAC secret) so it didn't cause the crash directly, but it was a real security hole: anyone with .env.example access could forge dev TURN credentials. (b) `coturn/coturn:latest` image dropped the `--no-tlsv1_1` command-line flag ‚Äî modern coturn uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` instead of the legacy deny-list `--no-tlsv1_X` scheme. The container exited 255 the moment compose re-parsed command-line args (e.g. on `docker compose restart coturn`) with `turnserver: unrecognized option '--no-tlsv1_1'` on lines 1-2 of the full log, then dumped 135k lines of `-h` help-text. Critical diagnostic discovery: `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text ‚Äî must use `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` for coturn flag crashes. Bug A alone was a latent vulnerability; bug B was the active crash. Both required fixing in this ticket ‚Äî neither fix alone would unblock WebRTC. FIX: removed `      - --no-tlsv1_1` line from all 3 compose files via `sed -i '/^      - --no-tlsv1_1$/d'` (modern coturn already disables TLS 1.0/1.1 by default ‚Äî removal is functionally equivalent to the legacy behavior); generated 3 independent 48-char base64-stripped openssl values (DEV / STAGING / PROD), patched all 3 env files in-place via sed (verified length=48 / all 3 unique / placeholder string count=0 after); added new `## üîÅ Coturn (WebRTC TURN/STUN Server) ‚úÖ FILLED` section to CREDENTIALS.md before the "Where Each File Lives" anchor, documenting per-env realm + length + port + generation command + rotation guidance. Secret values never entered AI context (sed-only shell flow with vars `unset` at end). VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev up -d` brought the full stack up cleanly (first attempt with `docker compose up -d coturn --force-recreate` against standalone media.yml failed with `service "livekit" depends on undefined service "valkey": invalid compose project` ‚Äî media.yml's livekit references valkey via depends_on, requires cache.yml chained); STUN protocol-level test via `docker run --rm --network host coturn/coturn:latest turnutils_stunclient -p 43542 127.0.0.1` returned `IPv4. UDP reflexive addr: 172.25.0.1:49796` (PASS); container `status=running, restarts=0, exitCode=0` stable for 30+ seconds; ports verified mapped 43542‚Üí3478 on TCP and UDP, IPv4 and IPv6. No source-code changes ‚Üí skipped pnpm typecheck/lint/test/build per blast-radius minimum (YAML-only diff, no TypeScript imports touched anywhere); validated via direct functional STUN test instead. Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS ‚Äî original ticket ask "fill credential, restart container" satisfied AND the deprecated-flag fix was in-scope-by-necessity (without it, credential fix accomplishes nothing); Stage 2 quality PASS ‚Äî 3-line YAML scope, zero source-code changes, secret values never entered AI context, conventional commit message ahead. 1 new typed lesson logged: üü° [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] documenting (a) the flag-rename pattern (deny-list ‚Üí minimum-version), (b) the critical `docker logs | head` diagnostic, (c) the recognition heuristic ("when coturn restart-loops with empty `docker logs --tail`, ALWAYS check `head` ‚Äî the actual error is on line 1-2 but coturn writes ~135k lines of help-text after"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries ‚Äî defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: coturn live and serving STUN/TURN on port 43542; (coturn-config-fix) ticket CLOSED. WebRTC peer connection now functional on localhost ‚Äî meeting #3 from Rule 16 cleanup (which previously PARTIAL'd because "WebRTC peer connection fails on coturn restarting 255") is unblocked. (rule-16-followup-multi-user) ‚Äî e2e re-smoke for #14/#15/#16 ‚Äî is no longer blocked from the coturn dimension; still needs multi-browser rig per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

LAST_DONE_TWO_PRIOR_X: 2026-05-19 Task #21 (admin-users-list-tenant-scope) ‚Äî Tier 2 inline Opus 4.7 controller. Confirmed Rule 16 smoke finding was a real cross-org leak NOT intentional super-admin visibility. Root cause was `packages/db/src/client.ts:44` L6 tenant-guard extension `if (!ctx || ctx.isSuperAdmin) return query(args)` bypass ‚Äî every tenant_admin holding `is_super_admin=true` silently skipped tenant filtering on EVERY route using the guarded prisma client. Fix two-layer: (a) ROOT ‚Äî removed `ctx.isSuperAdmin` bypass at L6 layer, only `!ctx` pass-through remains for bootstrap/seed scripts; dropped vestigial `isSuperAdmin` field from `TenantContext` interface and from the `runWithTenantContext` payload in trpc.ts. (b) DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH ‚Äî added explicit `where: { organization_id: ctx.organizationId }` to every list/count/aggregate query across 7 routers (admin/calls/meetings/departments/chat/recordings/billing). 6 new cross-org isolation tests on admin.test.ts (now 173/173 total). Squash-merged to main as `ab711da` + governance record `ddcbcad`. 1 new typed lesson: üî¥ [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]].

LESSONS_ADDED (1 this session):
  üü° [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] ‚Äî `coturn/coturn:latest` dropped `--no-tlsv1_1` flag (modern image uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` not legacy deny-list). When this flag is passed, turnserver exits 255 and dumps full `-h` help-text ‚Äî but `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text, the actual error is always on line 1-2. Recognition heuristic for future coturn debugging: `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` not `tail`. Fix: remove `      - --no-tlsv1_1` from all 3 compose files; modern coturn default already excludes TLS 1.0/1.1 so removal is functionally equivalent. Related: [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] ‚Äî both bugs caused dev WebRTC to fail silently, both diagnosed via Rule 16 cleanup pass.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 1 ‚Äî lightweight. Per memory-governance ¬ß1: scope was effectively 1 module (coturn config) + 3 envs of the same change. Score: (3 files √ó 2.5) + (1 module √ó 5) + (1 depth √ó 3) = 15.5. Well under Tier 2 threshold of 25. Inline controller was the right call given (a) the fix itself was YAML-only with no TypeScript imports anywhere, (b) sequential diagnostic work (find placeholder ‚Üí discover flag deprecation ‚Üí verify restart) doesn't parallelize, (c) subagent dispatch overhead would have far exceeded the saving on a 3-line patch.
